title: Incident Response Plan Tabletop and Live Test Execution Logging

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 12.10.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 12.10.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.84: Incident Response Plan Tabletop and Live Test Execution Logging.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.84' running on index=grc sourcetype="grc:ir_test" — test_id, scenario, executed_date, result, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype="grc:ir_test" earliest=-400d
| where match(scenario,"(?i)payment|breach|PAN|CDE|PCI)")
| stats latest(executed_date) as last_run, latest(result) as outcome by test_id
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_run,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,1)
| where days_since>365 OR isnull(last_run)
| table test_id, last_run, days_since, outcome
| sort days_since
description: Indexes IR test outcomes and scenario coverage so annual (or more frequent) response exercises for payment breaches are tracked with timestamps, participants, and lessons learned for PCI readiness reviews.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes grc:ir_test. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Push test completion from GRC after each tabletop. (2) Adjust `days_since` threshold if policy requires semi-annual tests. (3) Link `test_id` to remediation tickets. (4) Board briefing attachment optional. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=grc:ir_test`.
visualization: Timeline (last_run), table (overdue tests), single value (tests past due).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"` — `test_id`, `scenario`, `executed_date`, `result`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Push test completion from GRC after each tabletop. (2) Adjust `days_since` threshold if policy requires semi-annual tests. (3) Link `test_id` to remediation tickets. (4) Board briefing attachment optional.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:ir_test" earliest=-400d
| where match(scenario,"(?i)payment|breach|PAN|CDE|PCI)")
| stats latest(executed_date) as last_run, latest(result) as outcome by test_id
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_run,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,1)
| where days_since>365 OR isnull(last_run)
| table test_id, last_run, days_since, outcome
| sort days_since
```

Understanding this SPL

**Incident Response Plan Tabletop and Live Test Execution Logging (PCI DSS Req 12.10.1, 12.10.2)** — Indexes IR test outcomes and scenario coverage so annual (or more frequent) response exercises for payment breaches are tracked with timestamps, participants, and lessons learned for PCI readiness reviews.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"` — `test_id`, `scenario`, `executed_date`, `result`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: grc:ir_test. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="grc:ir_test", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(scenario,"(?i)payment|breach|PAN|CDE|PCI)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **test_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_since>365 OR isnull(last_run)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: days_since, last_run, outcome, test_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (last_run), table (overdue tests), single value (tests past due).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for indexes ir test outcomes and scenario coverage so annual (or more frequent) response exercises for payment breaches are tracked with timestamps, participants, and lessons learned for pci readiness. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
